Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class VA2MBus

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

VA2M-specific system bus that coordinates CPU, memory, and I/O operations for Apple IIe emulation.

public sealed class VA2MBus : IAppleIIBus, IBus, IDisposable
Inheritance
object
VA2MBus
Implements
IAppleIIBus
IBus
IDisposable
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Responsibilities:

  • CPU connection and cycle execution
  • Memory read/write routing to MemoryPool
  • I/O space address decoding ($C000-$CFFF)
  • Soft switch management and coordination
  • Language card banking logic
  • Keyboard input handling
  • Game controller pushbutton management
  • VBlank timing and event generation (~60 Hz)

Threading Model: All public methods (Connect, CpuRead/Write, Clock, Reset) must be called ONLY from the emulator worker thread. The VBlank event is raised on the emulator thread; UI subscribers MUST marshal to UI thread via dispatcher.

VBlank Timing: Emits VBlank event every 17,063 cycles (~60 Hz at 1.023 MHz), matching the Apple IIe NTSC vertical blanking interval. The VBlank blackout period lasts 4,550 cycles during which RD_VERTBLANK_ ($C019) reads as $80.

Disposal: After disposal, no further events are raised and Clock() becomes a no-op. Proper disposal prevents event handler leaks and ensures clean shutdown.

⚠️ PLANNED FOR REFACTORING: See Pandowdy.EmuCore/_docs_/VA2MBus-Refactoring-Notes.md for planned separation of concerns (slot bus system, floating bus emulation, etc.).

Constructors

VA2MBus(MemoryPool, ISystemStatusProvider, ICpu)

Initializes a new instance of the VA2MBus class.

public VA2MBus(MemoryPool mempool, ISystemStatusProvider statusProvider, ICpu cpu)

Parameters

mempool MemoryPool

Memory pool managing 128KB Apple IIe memory space.

statusProvider ISystemStatusProvider

System status provider implementing ISoftSwitchResponder for status updates.

cpu ICpu

CPU instance (6502 emulator) to connect to this bus.

Remarks

Initialization Sequence:

  1. Store dependency references
  2. Initialize I/O read handler dictionary (InitIoReadHandlers)
  3. Initialize I/O write handler dictionary (InitIoWriteHandlers)
  4. Register MemoryPool as soft switch responder (for memory remapping)
  5. Register SystemStatusProvider as soft switch responder (for UI updates)

I/O Handler Dictionaries: The bus maintains two dictionaries mapping I/O addresses to handler functions. This provides efficient dispatch and clear organization of I/O space behavior.

Exceptions

ArgumentNullException

Thrown if any parameter is null.

Properties

Cpu

Gets the CPU instance connected to this bus.

public ICpu Cpu { get; }

Property Value

ICpu

RAM

Gets the RAM (MemoryPool) for direct memory access.

public IMemory RAM { get; }

Property Value

IMemory

Remarks

Primarily used for testing and direct memory inspection. Normal CPU access should go through CpuRead/CpuWrite which handle I/O space routing.

Switches

Gets the soft switches managing memory mapping, video modes, ROM selection, and annunciators.

public SoftSwitches Switches { get; }

Property Value

SoftSwitches

Remarks

Provides access to the soft switch collection for querying states, registering responders, and debugging. The MemoryPool and SystemStatusProvider are automatically registered as responders during construction.

SystemClockCounter

Gets the total number of CPU cycles executed since last reset.

public ulong SystemClockCounter { get; }

Property Value

ulong

Methods

Clock()

Executes one CPU clock cycle and updates VBlank timing.

public void Clock()

Remarks

Execution Sequence:

  1. Execute one CPU instruction (CPU.Clock)
  2. Increment system clock counter
  3. Decrement VBlank blackout counter
  4. Check if VBlank cycle reached, fire event if so

VBlank Timing: When _systemClock reaches _nextVblankCycle (initially 12,480, then 29,510, 46,540, ...), the VBlank event is fired and _nextVblankCycle is advanced by CyclesPerVBlank (17,030). The VBlank blackout counter is reset to 4,550 cycles.

Frame Cycle Calculation: 

Frame 0: VBlank at cycle 12,480 (scanline 192)
Frame 1: VBlank at cycle 29,510 (12,480 + 17,030)
Frame 2: VBlank at cycle 46,540 (29,510 + 17,030)
...and so on

Catch-Up Logic: If the emulator runs fast (unthrottled batches), multiple VBlank cycles may be skipped. The do-while loop ensures _nextVblankCycle catches up to _systemClock, preventing event spam while maintaining proper phase alignment.

80-Column Synchronization: By firing VBlank at cycle 12,480 (not 0), we maintain synchronization with Apple IIe 80-column firmware which rapidly toggles PAGE2 during visible scanlines and relies on VBlank flag ($C019) for timing.

Disposal Safety: If the bus has been disposed, Clock() returns immediately without executing CPU or firing events.

Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only. VBlank event subscribers must marshal to UI thread if needed.

Connect(CPU)

Legacy CPU connection method. Not supported in VA2MBus.

public void Connect(CPU _)

Parameters

_ CPU

CPU instance (ignored).

Remarks

VA2MBus uses constructor injection for the CPU connection. The Connect method is deprecated and should not be called. This method exists only for IAppleIIBus interface compatibility.

Exceptions

NotSupportedException

Always thrown. VA2MBus requires CPU in constructor.

CpuRead(ushort, bool)

Reads a byte from memory or I/O space, routing to appropriate handler.

public byte CpuRead(ushort address, bool readOnly = false)

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit address to read from ($0000-$FFFF).

readOnly bool

If true, indicates a non-mutating read (not currently used).

Returns

byte

Byte value from memory or I/O space.

Remarks

Routing:

  • $C000-$C0FF: I/O space (ReadFromIOSpace)
  • All other addresses: MemoryPool.Read (RAM/ROM)

Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only.

Exceptions

ObjectDisposedException

Thrown if the bus has been disposed.

CpuWrite(ushort, byte)

Writes a byte to memory or I/O space, routing to appropriate handler.

public void CpuWrite(ushort address, byte data)

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit address to write to ($0000-$FFFF).

data byte

Byte value to write.

Remarks

Routing:

  • $C000-$C0FF: I/O space (WriteToIOSpace)
  • All other addresses: MemoryPool.Write (RAM, respecting write protection)

Write Protection: The MemoryPool enforces write protection based on soft switch states (ROM areas, language card write enable, etc.).

Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only.

Exceptions

ObjectDisposedException

Thrown if the bus has been disposed.

Dispose()

Disposes the bus, cleaning up resources and preventing further operation.

public void Dispose()

Remarks

Cleanup Operations:

  • Set _disposed flag to true
  • Clear VBlank event subscribers (prevent event handler leaks)

Post-Disposal Behavior:

  • Clock() becomes a no-op (returns immediately)
  • CpuRead/CpuWrite throw ObjectDisposedException
  • VBlank event is never fired again

Idempotent: Multiple calls to Dispose() are safe (no-op after first call).

Thread Safety: Should be called from the thread that owns the bus instance (typically after stopping emulation).

GetPushButton(int)

Gets the current state of a pushbutton.

public bool GetPushButton(int num)

Parameters

num int

Button number (0-2).

Returns

bool

True if button is pressed, false if released or invalid button number.

Remarks

Button states are readable at I/O addresses $C061-$C063 with bit 7 set when pressed.

Reset()

Performs a full system reset (power cycle).

public void Reset()

Remarks

Reset Operations:

  1. Reset memory ranges (MemoryPool.ResetRanges)
  2. Reset all soft switches to power-on state
  3. Reset CPU (PC loaded from $FFFC/$FFFD, SP = $FF)
  4. Reset system clock to zero
  5. Reset VBlank cycle counter

Difference from UserReset: Reset() is a cold boot that clears everything. UserReset() is a warm reset that preserves more state.

Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only.

Exceptions

ObjectDisposedException

Thrown if the bus has been disposed.

SetKeyValue(byte)

Sets the keyboard latch value as if a key was pressed.

public void SetKeyValue(byte key)

Parameters

key byte

ASCII key value with bit 7 set (indicating key available).

Remarks

Keyboard Latch: The Apple IIe keyboard latch appears at $C000. When a key is pressed, the ASCII value with bit 7 set appears at this address. Software reads $C010 (KEYSTRB) to clear bit 7, indicating the key has been read.

Thread Safety: Should be called only from the emulator thread. External threads should enqueue via VA2M.InjectKey().

SetPushButton(int, bool)

Sets the state of a pushbutton.

public void SetPushButton(int num, bool pressed)

Parameters

num int

Button number (0-2).

pressed bool

True if button is pressed, false if released.

Remarks

Thread Safety: Should be called only from the emulator thread. External threads should enqueue via VA2M.SetPushButton().

Invalid button numbers are silently ignored.

UserReset()

Performs a warm reset (Ctrl+Reset) without fully clearing state.

public void UserReset()

Remarks

Reset Operations:

  1. Reset all soft switches to power-on state
  2. Reset memory ranges (MemoryPool.ResetRanges)
  3. Reset CPU (PC loaded from $FFFC/$FFFD)
  4. Reset system clock to zero
  5. Reset VBlank cycle counter

Difference from Reset: In current implementation, UserReset() and Reset() are nearly identical. Future enhancements may preserve more state during UserReset (e.g., RAM contents, some soft switches) to better match Apple IIe Ctrl+Reset behavior.

Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only.

Exceptions

ObjectDisposedException

Thrown if the bus has been disposed.

Events

VBlank

Event raised every ~60 Hz (17,063 cycles) to signal vertical blanking interval.

public event EventHandler? VBlank

Event Type

EventHandler

Remarks

Timing: Fires at 1,023,000 Hz / 17,063 cycles ≈ 60.02 Hz (NTSC).

Thread Context: Always raised on the emulator thread. UI subscribers must marshal to UI thread via Dispatcher.BeginInvoke or similar.

Use Cases:

  • Frame rendering synchronization
  • Cursor blink timing
  • Status snapshot generation
  • Audio buffer flushing
Edit this page
Made with docfx
